prompt.task: Write a long-form content piece that dives deep into the topic below. The piece should be rigorous, coherent, and concrete.
prompt.topic: Bottom-of-Funnel Content: Why Most B2B SaaS Companies Get It Wrong
prompt.audience: B2B SaaS marketing leaders and content strategists

source_policy.policy: Draw on observable patterns in B2B SaaS marketing, not proprietary data. Use hypothetical examples based on common scenarios.
Acknowledge that what works varies by market, ACV, and sales cycle. Avoid universal prescriptions.

constraints.constraint: Start by defining BOFU content and why it matters to revenue.
Use the "buyer's remaining questions" framework to organize the analysis.
Include specific content formats with examples (not just "case studies").
Provide a scoring rubric for evaluating existing BOFU content.
Address the sales-marketing alignment problem explicitly.
Introduce key terms (BOFU, MQL, SQL, ACV) before using them.
Distinguish between correlation and causation in content performance.
Avoid name-dropping; only cite concepts/people if they add explanatory value.
Use the exact section headings specified in output_contract.
No preamble, no meta-commentary about the prompt.
Keep paragraphs reasonably short; prefer clarity over density.

prompt.optional_context: The reader's company likely has plenty of top-of-funnel content (blogs, podcasts) but struggles to convert qualified leads. Sales complains that leads "aren't ready." The reader needs to build a case for investing in BOFU content to their CMO.

reader_promises.promise: The reader can identify gaps in their current BOFU content strategy.
The reader has 5 specific content formats to evaluate for their context.
The reader can build a business case for BOFU content investment.

output_contract.format: Use markdown with H2 section headings exactly as written below. Under each heading, write substantive content (not bullet-only sections).

required_headings.heading: Introduction and explanation
Technique deep dive
Pros and cons, when and where to use
Use cases and examples
Applicability and potential
What's next

requirements.item: Define BOFU content and its role in the buyer journey
Explain why companies under-invest (easier metrics at top of funnel)
The five questions buyers have before signing (with content that answers each)
Why "case studies" often fail (and what to do instead)
The technical evaluation content gap (ROI calculators, implementation guides)
Common failure modes: too salesy, too vague, wrong stakeholder
When BOFU investment has highest ROI (long sales cycles, high ACV)
When TOFU focus makes more sense (volume plays, self-serve)
Anti-patterns: gating everything, ignoring sales feedback
Example 1: Comparison page that actually converts (structure breakdown)
Example 2: Technical implementation guide as deal accelerator
Edge case: BOFU content for multi-stakeholder buying committees
BOFU content scoring rubric (5 criteria with rating scale)
How to align with sales on content priorities
Metrics that actually matter (influenced pipeline, not pageviews)
Open questions: attribution and measuring true impact

output_contract.length: Aim for 2500-3500 words. Prefer completeness over brevity, but avoid filler.
